summary: This Vision Zero indicator tracks DOT's installation of Accessible Pedestrian Signals (APS), which send audible and vibrotactile crossing signals for blind and low-vision pedestrians. Installation pace is governed by annual targets under a Federal Court Remedial Order stemming from a December 2021 disability-rights lawsuit (American Council of the Blind of New York v. NYC DOT) that requires the City to equip 10,000 intersections with APS by the end of 2031, so year-to-year counts move with that court-ordered schedule rather than purely reflecting City priorities; DOT reports it fell short of its own FY25 target of 900.

sources.label: NYC Department of Transportation, Fiscal 2024 Mayor's Management Report
NYC Department of Transportation, Fiscal 2025 Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: increasing under a federal court-ordered installation schedule through 2031
